name: Ambiguous: PLATINUM at 36h deadline + comfortable stock cover + reliable alt (act-vs-wait)
rubric_notes: PLATINUM customer at 36h deadline — NOT <24h, so Rule 3 doesn't fire (rule 3 is for the within-24h panic). Stock cover (240h) >> delay (120h) — no catastrophic trigger. But PLATINUM at 36h is real exposure: their order is fulfillable from current stock with no margin for error. Rule 5 fires: PLATINUM at material risk (36 < 120) + reliable LOW alt → partial_order canonical. Three actions are defensible: wait_and_monitor (cover is comfortable; PLATINUM order fulfills from stock as long as primary doesn't slip further), immediate_switch (insurance for the PLATINUM relationship outweighs the 18% premium), partial_order (hedge — split the order so a slip doesn't lose the customer). Severity HIGH per §3 'HIGH-tier customer at risk in <48h'. Confidence MEDIUM if the Arbiter surfaces the tradeoff cleanly; LOW if it acknowledges the three-way ambiguity. This is the centerpiece scenario for testing swing_condition quality.
